analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-434

POL-434.title: Implement: Reconcile route-local POLARIS.md generation and drift detection
POL-434.body:
## Objective

Consolidate and complete Polaris's route-local `POLARIS.md` generation and reconciliation mechanism so that stale, missing, or drifted `POLARIS.md`/`SUMMARY.md` files can be detected and repaired safely across the whole repository in a single execution, without silently overwriting hand-authored content.

## Context

[POL-433](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-433/analyze-canonical-polarismd-reconciliation-and-route-local-doctrine>) (Analyze) found that route-local `POLARIS.md` generation is not a single ungoverned gap — three separate generators already exist (`resolveInstructionFile` discovery, `seed-instructions.ts`'s marker-respecting draft generator, and `adopt-canon.ts`'s LLM-driven enrichment), plus a partially-specified `polaris-reconcile` / `polaris-catalog` agent skill and a narrow `canon-check.ts` staleness detector. However:

* `adopt-canon.ts`'s `enrichCanonFiles()` unconditionally overwrites an existing `POLARIS.md` with LLM output with no check for the `<!-- polaris:draft -->` marker that every other generator respects — a hand-authored file it reaches will silently be replaced by every `polaris adopt` run (`src/cli/adopt-canon.ts:277-286`, confirmed by test `src/cli/adopt-canon.test.ts:68-83,125-131` asserting the overwrite happens).
* `canon-check.ts`'s doctrine-conflict detector reads `docs/doctrine/active` and `docs/specs/{active,implemented}` (`src/smartdocs-engine/canon-check.ts:68,84`), directories that do not exist in this repo — the real doctrine tree is `smartdocs/doctrine/...` and `smartdocs/specs/...` (both confirmed present). The detector silently finds zero canon files and never flags a real conflict.
* No content-hash or template-version fingerprint exists anywhere — staleness detection today is entirely git-log-count and file-timestamp heuristics, so there is no way to tell that a generated file was produced by an older template version versus one a human has since customized.
* There is no protected-region convention distinguishing Polaris-generated content from user-added content inside an already-promoted `POLARIS.md` — the only lifecycle marker (`DRAFT_MARKER`) is whole-file (draft vs. reviewed), not sub-section.
* The standalone `polaris-reconcile` / `polaris-catalog` skills document a `ReconcilePacket` schema (`affected_folders`, `work_inventory`, `allowed_write_paths`, `prohibited_write_paths`, `constraints`) that the real `polaris skill packet reconcile` bootloader never produces — it returns the generic `SkillPacket` shape instead, so an agent running the skill standalone today has no runtime-authoritative source for those fields.
* No command performs a full-repo one-shot sweep that repairs (not just creates) drifted `POLARIS.md`/`SUMMARY.md` content; `seedInstructionsAll()` only fills in missing files.

Source analysis: `smartdocs/raw/pol-433-polaris-md-reconciliation-analysis.md`

## Goal

After this cluster:

1. `polaris adopt` never overwrites a non-draft `POLARIS.md`.
2. `canon-check.ts` reads the real doctrine/specs directories and its conflict detection is not a silent no-op.
3. Every Polaris-generated `POLARIS.md`/`SUMMARY.md` wraps its generated content in `<!-- BEGIN POLARIS GENERATED -->` / `<!-- END POLARIS GENERATED -->` markers, and both generators share one `SUMMARY.md` template.
4. Every generated region carries a template-version stamp, and `validate-instructions.ts` can detect stamp-based drift independent of timestamps.
5. `polaris skill packet reconcile` returns a real `ReconcilePacket` with populated `affected_folders`/`work_inventory` derived from git diff + `.polaris/map/file-routes.json`.
6. A new one-shot command sweeps the whole repo, regenerating only the GENERATED region of drifted files, creating missing files, and reporting files it cannot safely touch — this is also the migration path for files already in draft state and any hand-authored files needing marker adoption.

## Scope

(cluster-wide, for documentation only — each child defines its own authoritative `## Scope`)

* src/cli/adopt-canon.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/canon-check.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/triage.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/validate-instructions.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/index.ts
* src/skill-packet/generator.ts
* src/skill-packet/types.ts
* smartdocs/doctrine/active/smartdocs-summary-architecture.md

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 6 children Done
- [ ] `npm run build` and `npm test` pass at cluster completion
- [ ] No existing non-draft POLARIS.md is overwritten by `polaris adopt`
- [ ] The one-shot reconciliation sweep can be run once on this repo and produces a clean report

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test

## Ordering

Six children — see individual `## Ordering` sections. Children fixing the overwrite bug, the doctrine path bug, the GENERATED-region template, and the ReconcilePacket wiring are independent and can run in parallel; the fingerprint child depends on the GENERATED-region child; the full-repo sweep depends on both the GENERATED-region and fingerprint children.

## Non-goals

* Do not change the Linear tracker adapter or any tracker-specific logic.
* Do not implement the LLM-mediated `adopt-canon` enrichment quality (prompt content) — only its overwrite safety.
* Do not migrate the `docs/Polaris/` legacy layout (separate, already-scoped concern per `smartdocs/specs/active/docs-authority-model.md`).

POL-433.title: Analyze: Canonical POLARIS.md reconciliation and route-local doctrine generation

POL-440.title: Implement: One-shot repo-wide POLARIS.md/SUMMARY.md reconciliation sweep
POL-440.body:
## Objective

Add a single command that scans every routed folder in the repository and repairs missing or drifted `POLARIS.md`/`SUMMARY.md` GENERATED regions in one execution.

## Context

[POL-433](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-433/analyze-canonical-polarismd-reconciliation-and-route-local-doctrine>) (Analyze) found that no existing command performs a repair sweep: `seedInstructionsAll()` (`seed-instructions.ts`) only creates files that don't exist yet; it never touches an existing file, drifted or not. The issue's stated goal of "a one-shot reconciliation process that can detect and repair stale or invalid POLARIS.md files across an entire repository" has no implementation today. This child is also the migration path for the repo's current mixed state: files still carrying `DRAFT_MARKER` can be safely regenerated, while promoted files newly wrapped with GENERATED markers (via [POL-437](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-437/implement-protected-generated-regions-and-a-single-shared>)) can have their generated region refreshed when the [POL-439](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-439/implement-template-version-fingerprint-for-generated-region-drift>) template-version stamp is stale.

## Goal

A new command (e.g. `polaris docs reconcile --all`) walks every route in `.polaris/map/file-routes.json`, and for each folder's `POLARIS.md`/`SUMMARY.md`:

* Missing → generate via the existing seed path.
* Present, contains `DRAFT_MARKER` → regenerate in full (current seed behavior).
* Present, has a GENERATED region whose template-version stamp is stale → regenerate only the content inside the GENERATED markers, leaving everything outside untouched.
* Present, no GENERATED markers and no `DRAFT_MARKER` (pre-[POL-437](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-437/implement-protected-generated-regions-and-a-single-shared-summarymd) hand-authored file) → skip, and report it as needing manual migration.

The command prints a summary (counts of created/regenerated/skipped/blocked, with paths) and exits non-zero only on an unhandled error, not on skips.

## Scope

* src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/index.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `polaris docs reconcile --all` runs once against this repo without error and produces a report
- [ ] Missing files are created; drifted GENERATED regions are regenerated in place with non-GENERATED content preserved byte-for-byte
- [ ] Pre-[POL-437](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-437/implement-protected-generated-regions-and-a-single-shared-summarymd) hand-authored files (no markers) are left untouched and listed as needing manual migration
- [ ] `--dry-run` prints the same report without writing any files
- [ ] Tests pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/smartdocs-engine

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-437](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-437/implement-protected-generated-regions-and-a-single-shared>) and [POL-439](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-439/implement-template-version-fingerprint-for-generated-region-drift>) being Done first.

## Non-goals

* Do not auto-migrate pre-[POL-437](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-437/implement-protected-generated-regions-and-a-single-shared-summarymd) hand-authored files by guessing where markers should go — report them as blocked instead.
* Do not change `polaris welfare-check`.
* Do not wire this command into `polaris adopt` or `polaris init` automatically in this child (future work, out of scope here).

POL-439.title: Implement: Template-version fingerprint for GENERATED region drift detection
POL-439.body:
## Objective

Give every GENERATED region a template-version stamp so drift from the current template can be detected deterministically, independent of file timestamps.

## Context

[POL-433](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-433/analyze-canonical-polarismd-reconciliation-and-route-local-doctrine>) (Analyze) found that all staleness detection today is heuristic: `validate-instructions.ts` flags a directory as stale only if ≥3 files changed since the POLARIS.md's last git-modified date, and `welfare.ts` uses a 30-day `last_updated` threshold. Neither can tell whether a file's GENERATED region (introduced by [POL-437](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-437/implement-protected-generated-regions-and-a-single-shared>) in this cluster) was produced by the current template or an older one — e.g. after [POL-437](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-437/implement-protected-generated-regions-and-a-single-shared-summarymd) lands and the template gains a ruling on the "Route model" section, existing generated files would have no way to signal they predate that change.

## Goal

1. `seed-instructions.ts` defines a `TEMPLATE_VERSION` constant and embeds it as a stamp (e.g. `<!-- polaris:template-version: N -->`) inside the GENERATED region produced by `generateDraft()`/`generateSummaryDraft()`.
2. `validate-instructions.ts` gains a check that reads the embedded stamp and reports drift when it does not match the current `TEMPLATE_VERSION`, in addition to (not replacing) the existing git-log heuristic.

## Scope

* src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.test.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/validate-instructions.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/validate-instructions.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `TEMPLATE_VERSION` constant exists and is embedded in every newly generated GENERATED region
- [ ] `validate-instructions.ts` detects and reports a version mismatch between an embedded stamp and the current `TEMPLATE_VERSION`
- [ ] Files with no stamp (pre-[POL-437](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-437/implement-protected-generated-regions-and-a-single-shared-summarymd) files) are reported as "unstamped" rather than crashing or false-passing
- [ ] Tests pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/smartdocs-engine

## Ordering

Depends on [POL-437](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-437/implement-protected-generated-regions-and-a-single-shared>) being Done first (GENERATED region must exist before it can be stamped).

## Non-goals

* Do not implement the repo-wide sweep that acts on drift (covered by a sibling child).
* Do not change the git-log-based heuristic already in `validate-instructions.ts`.

POL-438.title: Implement: Populate real ReconcilePacket fields in the reconcile skill packet bootloader
POL-438.body:
## Objective

Make `polaris skill packet reconcile` return the `ReconcilePacket` schema the `polaris-reconcile`/`polaris-catalog` skills document, instead of the generic `SkillPacket`.

## Context

[POL-433](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-433/analyze-canonical-polarismd-reconciliation-and-route-local-doctrine>) (Analyze) found that `.polaris/skills/polaris-reconcile/SKILL.md:59-71` documents a `ReconcilePacket` with `affected_folders`, `work_inventory`, `allowed_write_paths`, `prohibited_write_paths`, and `constraints`, obtained via `polaris skill packet reconcile`. The actual bootloader (`src/skill-packet/generator.ts:383-411`) returns the generic `SkillPacket` shape (`src/skill-packet/types.ts:58-75`: `authority_boundaries`/`prohibited_actions`/`allowed_outputs`/`deliverables`/`stop_conditions`) — none of the documented fields are populated. A rich equivalent (`CloseoutLibrarianPacket`, `src/cognition/closeout-librarian-types.ts:72`) already exists but only fires when `state.status === "cluster-complete"` (`src/cognition/librarian-packet.ts:44`), which is unavailable for a standalone (non-closeout) reconcile invocation. As a result, an agent running `polaris-reconcile` standalone today has no runtime-authoritative source for `affected_folders`/`work_inventory` and must fabricate them from chat context — directly violating the skill's own stated rule that "chat reasoning is not authoritative" (`polaris-reconcile/chain.md:8-13`).

## Goal

`polaris skill packet reconcile` (standalone, non-closeout invocation) returns a packet containing `affected_folders` and `work_inventory` derived from git diff (uncommitted changes plus the current branch's diff against its merge-base with the default branch) cross-referenced against `.polaris/map/file-routes.json` route entries, plus `allowed_write_paths` ([POLARIS.md/SUMMARY.md](<http://POLARIS.md/SUMMARY.md>) only, within affected folders), `prohibited_write_paths` (everything else), and `constraints` (e.g. `max_summary_addition_lines`).

## Scope

* src/skill-packet/generator.ts
* src/skill-packet/types.ts
* src/skill-packet/generator.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `polaris skill packet reconcile` returns `affected_folders` and `work_inventory` populated from real git-diff + map data, not empty/placeholder values
- [ ] `allowed_write_paths` is restricted to [POLARIS.md/SUMMARY.md](<http://POLARIS.md/SUMMARY.md>) under affected folders; `prohibited_write_paths` covers everything else
- [ ] Falls back gracefully (explicit `blocked`/empty state, not a fabricated guess) when there is no git diff to inspect
- [ ] Tests pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/skill-packet

## Ordering

None — independent of all other children in this cluster.

## Non-goals

* Do not change the `CloseoutLibrarianPacket` cluster-closeout path.
* Do not change `polaris-reconcile/SKILL.md`'s documented schema shape.
* Do not implement the full-repo sweep (covered by a sibling child) — this child covers the agent-driven, diff-scoped path only.

POL-437.title: Implement: Protected GENERATED regions and a single shared SUMMARY.md template
POL-437.body:
## Objective

Give every Polaris-generated `POLARIS.md`/`SUMMARY.md` a marked, safely-regenerable region, and remove the duplicate `SUMMARY.md` scaffold template.

## Context

[POL-433](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-433/analyze-canonical-polarismd-reconciliation-and-route-local-doctrine>) (Analyze) found two gaps that block safe reconciliation:

1. The only lifecycle marker today is whole-file (`DRAFT_MARKER`, `<!-- polaris:draft -->`, `src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.ts:7`) — once a human removes the marker, the entire file becomes off-limits to any future automated update, even if most of its content is still boilerplate the tool generated. There is no way to regenerate just the machine-owned part of an already-reviewed file.
2. Two independent `SUMMARY.md` scaffold templates exist: `generateSummaryDraft()` in `src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.ts` and `scaffoldDraftSummaryFiles()` in `src/cli/adopt-canon.ts:32-46` (a much more minimal template: `"# ${folder}\n\n<!-- polaris:draft -->\n"`). They produce different output for the same conceptual operation depending on which code path runs first.

Also relevant: sampled route-local files show an organically-added, non-canonical 8th "## Route model" section in deeper `src/` folders (e.g. `src/loop/POLARIS.md:40-45`, `src/cli/POLARIS.md`) that isn't part of the 7-section template defined in `smartdocs/doctrine/active/smartdocs-summary-architecture.md:28-127`. This child's template work must explicitly decide whether "Route model" becomes an optional canonical 8th section or is left as free-form content outside the GENERATED region — it must not silently strip existing content.

## Goal

1. `generateDraft()` and `generateSummaryDraft()` (`seed-instructions.ts`) wrap their machine-populated body content in `<!-- BEGIN POLARIS GENERATED -->` / `<!-- END POLARIS GENERATED -->` markers.
2. `adopt-canon.ts` calls the shared `seed-instructions.ts` summary/POLARIS template functions instead of maintaining its own `scaffoldDraftSummaryFiles()`.
3. `smartdocs/doctrine/active/smartdocs-summary-architecture.md` is updated to document the GENERATED-region convention and to explicitly rule on the "Route model" section (canonize as optional 8th section, or document it as allowed free-form content outside the GENERATED region).
4. `validate-instructions.ts`'s role-boundary/section checks are updated so they do not misfire on content inside vs. outside the GENERATED region.

## Scope

* src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.test.ts
* src/cli/adopt-canon.ts
* src/cli/adopt-canon.test.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/validate-instructions.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/validate-instructions.test.ts
* smartdocs/doctrine/active/smartdocs-summary-architecture.md

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Generated `POLARIS.md`/`SUMMARY.md` content is wrapped in `<!-- BEGIN POLARIS GENERATED -->` / `<!-- END POLARIS GENERATED -->`
- [ ] `adopt-canon.ts` no longer defines its own SUMMARY.md scaffold template; it calls the shared generator
- [ ] Doctrine doc updated with the GENERATED-region convention and an explicit ruling on the "Route model" section
- [ ] `validate-instructions.ts` section/role-boundary checks pass on files containing GENERATED markers plus non-canonical trailing sections outside the markers
- [ ] Tests pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/smartdocs-engine src/cli/adopt-canon.test.ts

## Ordering

None — independent of all other children in this cluster. Two sibling children (template-version fingerprint, one-shot reconciliation sweep) depend on this one.

## Non-goals

* Do not retrofit GENERATED markers onto already-promoted, hand-authored POLARIS.md files (that is the one-shot sweep child's job).
* Do not change the LLM prompt content in `adopt-canon.ts`'s `dispatchCanonAgent`.
* Do not add fingerprint/version stamping (covered by a sibling child).

POL-436.title: Fix: Point canon-check doctrine/spec conflict detection at real smartdocs paths
POL-436.body:
## Objective

Fix `canon-check.ts`'s doctrine/spec conflict detector so it reads the doctrine and specs directories that actually exist in this repo, instead of silently finding nothing.

## Context

[POL-433](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-433/analyze-canonical-polarismd-reconciliation-and-route-local-doctrine>) (Analyze) confirmed `src/smartdocs-engine/canon-check.ts` builds its canon file set from `docs/doctrine/active` (line 68), `docs/specs/active` and `docs/specs/implemented` (line 84), and writes new candidate drafts to `docs/doctrine/candidate` (line 353) and stale-doc flags to `docs/raw` (line 307). None of these `docs/...` directories exist in this repo — verified: `docs/` contains only `reports/`, `superpowers/`, `SETUP.md`, `USAGE.md`. The real doctrine/specs tree is `smartdocs/doctrine/{active,candidate,deprecated}` and `smartdocs/specs/{active,implemented}` (both confirmed present), and the real raw-docs staging location is `smartdocs/raw/` (per `smartdocs/specs/active/docs-authority-model.md`). `src/smartdocs-engine/triage.ts:552-553` already uses the correct `smartdocs/doctrine/{active,candidate}` paths, confirming the `docs/`-prefixed paths in `canon-check.ts` are the outlier.

Because `existsSync(doctrineDir)` and `existsSync(specDir)` both return false against the wrong paths, the canon-conflict detector currently builds an empty canon set on every invocation — its modal-verb contradiction detection never fires, on any run, in this repo.

## Goal

`canon-check.ts` reads canon files from `smartdocs/doctrine/active`, `smartdocs/specs/active`, and `smartdocs/specs/implemented`, and writes candidate drafts / stale flags to `smartdocs/doctrine/candidate` and `smartdocs/raw` respectively.

## Scope

* src/smartdocs-engine/canon-check.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/canon-check.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All `docs/doctrine`, `docs/specs`, `docs/raw` path references in `canon-check.ts` are corrected to their `smartdocs/` equivalents
- [ ] A conflict-detection test using a real `smartdocs/doctrine/active` fixture with a contradicting statement produces a non-empty conflict result
- [ ] Existing canon-check tests updated/passing against the corrected paths
- [ ] Tests pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/smartdocs-engine/canon-check.test.ts

## Ordering

None — independent of all other children in this cluster.

## Non-goals

* Do not change `triage.ts` (already correct).
* Do not change the modal-verb contradiction heuristic itself.
* Do not migrate the historical `docs/Polaris/` layout (separate, already-scoped elsewhere).

POL-435.title: Fix: Guard adopt-canon POLARIS.md write against overwriting reviewed files
POL-435.body:
## Objective

Stop `polaris adopt`'s canon-enrichment phase from unconditionally overwriting an existing, human-reviewed `POLARIS.md`.

## Context

[POL-433](<https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-433/analyze-canonical-polarismd-reconciliation-and-route-local-doctrine>) (Analyze) found that `enrichCanonFiles()` in `src/cli/adopt-canon.ts` writes LLM-generated content to `POLARIS.md` unconditionally when the LLM response contains `polaris_lines`:

```ts
if ((response.polaris_lines ?? []).length > 0) {
  const polarisPath = join(dir, "POLARIS.md");
  ...
  writeFileSync(polarisPath, polarisContent, "utf-8");
}
```

(`src/cli/adopt-canon.ts:277-286`)

Every other generator in this codebase (`seed-instructions.ts`'s `seedInstructions()`) checks `existsSync` and skips when a file exists and doesn't contain `DRAFT_MARKER` (`<!-- polaris:draft -->`, defined at `src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.ts:7`). `adopt-canon.ts` has no such check. The existing test `src/cli/adopt-canon.test.ts:68-83,125-131` pre-writes a non-draft `POLARIS.md` (`"# POLARIS\n"`) and asserts it gets fully replaced — this test's expectation must be inverted as part of this fix.

## Goal

`enrichCanonFiles()` only writes `POLARIS.md` when the file does not exist, or exists and still contains `DRAFT_MARKER`. An existing file without the marker is left untouched and logged as skipped.

## Scope

* src/cli/adopt-canon.ts
* src/cli/adopt-canon.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `enrichCanonFiles()` checks for `DRAFT_MARKER` (or absence of the file) before writing `POLARIS.md`
- [ ] A pre-existing, non-draft `POLARIS.md` is never modified by `polaris adopt`
- [ ] A pre-existing draft-marked `POLARIS.md` may still be replaced by enrichment
- [ ] `src/cli/adopt-canon.test.ts` updated to assert preservation of non-draft files (its current assertion of full overwrite is corrected)
- [ ] Tests pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli/adopt-canon.test.ts

## Ordering

None — independent of all other children in this cluster.

## Non-goals

* Do not change the SUMMARY.md write path in this file (covered by the GENERATED-region child in this cluster).
* Do not change the LLM prompt or `polaris_lines` content generation logic.
* Do not add GENERATED-region markers here (covered by a sibling child).
